currentFacts: Electron desktop pet platform with Control Center
PetService is the single source of truth for pet state
Control Center owns user-facing configuration
API keys stay in the main-process secret store

Third-party ecosystem docs frame packages as local extensions with legacy SDK compatibility, normalized entries declarations, entries.commands support through the existing JavaScript compatibility runner, and honest non-sandbox language
Local HTTP and MCP are loopback-only and disabled by default
Codex pet directory and zip import are supported
Bundled pets include doro, duodong, and chispa

TypeScript migration baseline covers shared IPC, Control Center view contracts, the Control Center API facade, Control Center hook state boundaries, Control Center pane prop surfaces, main-process Control Center adapters for service/catalog/plugin/pet pack/About/update/actions payloads, plugin extension entry contracts, full release evidence archive / signed closure report contracts, and representative product payload fixtures

Packaged runtime smoke runner has archived real macOS packaged runtime evidence for automated launch, transparency, sprite, speech bubble, action playback, and built-in pack switching checks; desktop picker reports now include invalid-package feedback evidence, and native picker evidence is still pending

Release evidence archive manifest tooling and signed release closure reporting are available; the current archived closure report records official desktop, macOS, and Windows claims as not-ready until real signed evidence is complete
Current legacy plugin config is public settings; secret-like plugin config fields are rejected by the compatibility validator
Legacy SDK scaffolding is available through npm run create-openpet-plugin, including minimal, network, storage, and AI-assisted templates

Extension author docs are centered on plugin.json entries for commands, services, dashboards, manifest declarations, optional config, and assets; current runtime supports entries.commands only through JavaScript compatibility packages with main, while services and dashboards are visible declarations only
Plugin sandbox evaluation recommends keeping the current child-process Node permission-model VM runner for v1.1 while documenting that it is not an absolute sandbox
Installed user pet packs can be exported as .openpet-pet.zip and re-imported with provenance metadata
AI behavior decisions are visible in Control Center with replay, redacted diagnostics export, and clear-history controls
Live documentation has been consolidated so README, HANDOFF, project-status-review, project-context, and v1.1 TODO status share the same current facts
The latest review-derived TODO design lives in docs/productization-next-steps-design.md
The Phase 38+ execution design lives in docs/productization-v1.1-todo-design.md

releaseNotes.humanFacing: Keep README and CHANGELOG short, factual, and free of phase-by-phase history.
releaseNotes.machineFacing: Use this file plus docs/project-documentation-design.md for quick programmatic context.
